jobs.title: Staff Product Manager, Activation
jobs.description:
The Role

As Staff Product Manager, Activation at Linktree, you will own one of the most important moments in our customer journey: helping new users get set up, understand Linktree's value, and reach their first meaningful outcome as quickly as possible.

Linktree serves a massive global audience of creators, entrepreneurs, businesses, artists, publishers, and brands. Your work will shape how millions of new users move from sign-up to setup, from setup to sharing, and from sharing to seeing real audience engagement, growth, and monetization value.

This is a senior, high-impact product leadership role for someone who thinks in funnels, learns through experimentation, and can turn complex behavioral data into simple, high-converting product experiences. You will partner closely with Engineering, Design, Data, Lifecycle, Marketing, Revenue, and executive leadership to define what activation means at Linktree and build the systems that make it happen at scale.

What You Will Own

- Own Linktree's activation strategy across the early customer journey, from sign-up and account creation through setup, first publish, first share, first audience engagement, and repeat usage.
- Define the activation model for Linktree, including the key milestones, aha moments, leading indicators, and segment-specific paths that predict long-term retention and monetization.
- Build and continuously improve onboarding, setup, templates, recommendations, empty states, nudges, lifecycle touchpoints, and in-product guidance that help users reach value faster.
- Develop a high-velocity experimentation roadmap focused on improving setup completion, time to value, early engagement, D7/D30 retention, and downstream conversion.
- Lead deep funnel analysis to identify where users drop off, why they get stuck, and which interventions will create the highest impact.
- Partner with Data to ensure the activation funnel is well-instrumented, measurable, and understood across the company.
- Translate qualitative insight and behavioral data into sharp product hypotheses, clear priorities, and testable product bets.
- Work cross-functionally with Marketing, Lifecycle, Revenue, and Brand to ensure the journey from acquisition message to in-product experience feels cohesive and intentional.
- Communicate strategy, tradeoffs, experiment results, and business impact clearly to senior leadership and executive audiences.
- Raise the bar for growth product craft at Linktree by bringing strong experimentation discipline, funnel thinking, and customer empathy into the product organization.

What We Are Looking For

- 8+ years of product management experience, with significant experience in growth, activation, onboarding, product-led growth, lifecycle, or funnel optimization.
- Proven experience owning a large-scale consumer, prosumer, marketplace, creator, SaaS, or self-serve product funnel with meaningful user volume.
- A track record of improving activation, onboarding completion, retention, conversion, or time to value through product-led experimentation.
- Strong analytical depth: you are fluent in funnel analysis, cohort analysis, segmentation, experimentation design, and metric definition.
- Hands-on experience with product analytics and experimentation tools such as Amplitude, Mixpanel, Looker, Mode, Optimizely, Statsig, or similar.
- Excellent executive communication skills, including the ability to present complex data, crisp narratives, and strategic recommendations to senior leaders.
- Strong product judgment: you know when to optimize, when to rethink the journey, and when a small product change can unlock a much larger behavior shift.
- Experience partnering deeply with Design and Engineering to ship polished, scalable, user-centered product experiences.
- Comfort operating in ambiguity, creating clarity across teams, and influencing without direct authority.
- A strong customer instinct and curiosity about how creators, businesses, and brands use digital tools to grow their presence, audience, and income.

How You Work

- You are deeply data-driven, but not data-blind. You use numbers to find the problem, user research to understand it, and experimentation to learn what works.
- You love funnels. You naturally break journeys into stages, identify the conversion points that matter, and build a clear point of view on which moments deserve the team's attention.
- You are a strategic communicator. You can zoom into experiment design with a data scientist, unblock scope with engineers, sharpen a flow with design, and then walk an executive team through the business case with confidence.
- You move quickly, but with discipline. You know how to balance speed, craft, and signal quality so the team is not just shipping more experiments, but learning faster and making better decisions.
- You care about helping users succeed. At Linktree, activation is not just getting someone through a checklist. It is helping them create, share, grow, and see why Linktree belongs at the center of their digital universe.

Linktree is committed to providing a fair and competitive compensation package including cash, equity, and benefits. The base salary offered for this role is targeted at $215,000-$230,000 for roles based in San Francisco and Los Angeles. Final offers depend on multiple factors including location, experience, expertise, and role scope, and may vary from the range listed.

P.S. If you don’t tick every box in this listing, please don’t rule yourself out. We take pride in inclusion and hiring incredible human beings with great potential over ticking boxes – so if this role resonates with you, hit that apply button!

Our Story

We’re on a mission to empower anyone to curate, grow, and monetize their digital universe. We created the “link in bio” category — and we’re just getting started. From creators, artists, and entrepreneurs to global brands, we’re redefining how people connect with their communities, showcase their content and promote products — all in one link.

Today, a community of 80+ million use Linktree, including icons like Adidas, TikTok, the UN Environmental Program, The NFL, Manchester United, World Central Kitchen, Sabrina Carpenter, Olivia Rodrigo, and Selena Gomez. With 48,000 new accounts created every day, Linktree is the fastest-growing leader in our space.

We’ve teamed up with some of the world’s biggest platforms including TikTok, Snap, YouTube, GoFundMe, Spotify, Google, Stripe, Reddit, Laylo, and Kajabi, to unify Linkers’ digital spaces, and we partner with retail brands Amazon, Lululemon, Nike, Target, Sephora, Lululemon, and more to share products they love.

We’re a tight-knit, passionate team building best-in-class tools that make online presence simple, powerful, and yours. If you're excited by the idea of shaping a new category, setting the standard, and making space for everyone to be seen and succeed, we’d love to meet you.

Where and How We Work

We're a global, diverse team spread across continents with offices in London, Los Angeles, Melbourne, and San Francisco. We work together flexibly and you can choose the setup that works best for you: fully remote or a hybrid mix of office and home.

We offer autonomy in how you structure your days and weeks. While we’re mindful of time zones and occasionally collaborate outside the typical 9–5, we lean into async work to keep things flowing. Our culture is family-friendly and Zoom-casual where kids, pets, and delivery drivers are welcome.

How we’ll help you thrive

Our approach to benefits considers the whole person and the unique contributions they bring to Linktree. We want the experience at Linktree to be one that enables people to truly thrive so we can Go Further Together (one of our values). Some ways we support you:

- An annual wellbeing allowance to use on things like (but not limited to) fitness memberships, development courses, childcare, travel, charitable donations, pet insurance, home office set-up - the choice is yours!
- 100% coverage (and 80% for your dependents) of your monthly premiums for medical, dental, vision, disability and life insurance for US-based employees.
- Employer contribution towards your retirement.
- Generous time off for vacation, holidays, parental leave, volunteer time, and other categories.
- Employee stock option program - we want each and every employee to share in the company’s success.

At Linktree, we believe in promoting a culture that celebrates unique backgrounds, talents, and experiences, and we’re proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We are creating an inclusive workplace where every individual feels valued, respected, and has equal opportunities to thrive. We aim to foster a diverse and inclusive environment where all team members have a sense of belonging. Linktree welcomes all people regardless of sex, gender identity, race, ethnicity, disability, pregnancy, age, or other lived experience. If you require accommodations to fully participate in our opportunities, please don't hesitate to reach us at talent@linktr.ee – your needs are important to us.

jobs.title: Sr Administrative Support Spec
jobs.description:
WHO ARE WE?

- We are a long-standing organization dedicated to supporting grocery retailers across the Intermountain West for over 85 years.
- Our company prioritizes our team members, their families, and the communities we serve.
- We proudly partner with local organizations to fundraise, donate, and sponsor various charitable activities and events.

WHY JOIN THE TEAM?

- Comprehensive Benefits: Health, dental, vision, life insurance, 401(k), vacation, and sick leave for eligible team members. Some benefits have waiting periods (e.g., vacation accrual, 401(k), tuition reimbursement).
- Perks & Discounts: Team member perks and 10% off groceries.
- Flexible Work: Hybrid schedules available for select positions, combining in-office collaboration with remote flexibility.
- Tuition Reimbursement: Support for continued education.
- Career Growth: Opportunities for advancement within the company.
- Daily Pay: Access earned wages more frequently.

JOB SUMMARY: 
The Senior Administrative Support Specialist is a vital role responsible for the detailed setup and data entry of assigned category product information, and managing various assignments from the category manager. This position involves organizing workload, entering promotional information, updating pricing, and handling product data and clerical tasks. The Senior Specialist will also build and maintain strong relationships with vendors and team members across corporate departments and member stores through effective communication via phone, email, and in-person interactions.

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

- Provide comprehensive support to the category manager, vendor partners, and team members.
- Accurately enter product, promotional, and pricing information into the system.
- Communicate essential information effectively through phone, email, and face-to-face interactions.
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure alignment on product information and promotional activities.
- Perform additional duties and responsibilities as assigned by management.
- Regular on-site attendance is required to fulfill the responsibilities of this position.

POSITION REQUIREMENTS 

- Education/Training: High School Diploma or equivalent; additional education is a plus.
- Experience: 1+ year of data entry or retail store experience preferred.
- Skills/Knowledge:
- Strong customer service, accounting, and analytical skills.
- Exceptional verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to convey information clearly and effectively.
- Proven problem-solving and organizational skills, with a keen attention to detail.
- Proficient in the use of spreadsheets and databases, with strong keyboarding skills.
- Ability to manage multiple projects and tasks efficiently.
- Regular on-site attendance is required. This is a hybrid schedule after 90 days. 3 days in office and 2 remote days. 

This list of duties, responsibilities, and position requirements is not intended to be all-inclusive and may be expanded to include other duties or responsibilities that management may deem necessary.

PHYSICAL/SENSORY DEMANDS 

- Work is performed in an office setting, with exposure to computer screens, indoor lighting, and prolonged sitting.
- Regular on-site attendance is required.
- A personal vehicle may be needed for travel to retail locations. 

Associated Food Stores is an equal-opportunity employer. We prohibit unlawful discrimination against applicants or employees based on race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, or local laws.

jobs.title: Technical Product Marketer, k0rdent AI – remote in the US
jobs.description:
Company Description
Mirantis is the Kubernetes-native AI infrastructure company, enabling organizations to build and operate scalable, secure, and sovereign infrastructure for modern AI, machine learning, and data-intensive applications. By combining open source innovation with deep expertise in Kubernetes orchestration, Mirantis empowers platform engineering teams to deliver composable, production-ready developer platforms across any environment—on-premises, in the cloud, at the edge, or in sovereign data centers. As enterprises navigate the growing complexity of AI-driven workloads, Mirantis delivers the automation, GPU orchestration, and policy-driven control needed to manage infrastructure with confidence and agility. Committed to open standards and freedom from lock-in, Mirantis ensures that customers retain full control of their infrastructure strategy.

https://www.mirantis.com/

Job Description

- 
Develop and maintain core positioning, messaging frameworks, and value propositions for Mirantis’ AI infrastructure and cloud-native platform portfolio.

- 
Translate highly technical product capabilities — GPU cluster orchestration, multi-cluster Kubernetes management, MLOps pipelines — into audience-specific narratives for practitioners, platform engineers, and C-suite buyers.

- 
Own the competitive intelligence function: track competitors including Red Hat OpenShift AI, NVIDIA Base Command, Rancher/SUSE, VMware Tanzu, and emerging AI cloud providers; produce battlecards, comparisons, and win/loss analysis.

- 
Produce a steady cadence of high-quality technical content: solution briefs, white papers, blog posts, use case studies, reference architectures, and demo scripts.

- 
Partner with solution architects and product managers to develop technical content that educates buyers on AI infrastructure patterns, including LLM inference at scale, GPU scheduling, and developer platform self-service.

- 
Support demand generation campaigns with compelling assets aligned to key personas: AI/ML engineers, platform ops teams, and infrastructure decision-makers.

- 
Build and maintain sales enablement materials, including pitch decks, battlecards, objection-handling guides, and ROI frameworks.

- 
Partner with solution architects and sales teams to distill field learnings into sharper messaging and better-targeted content.

- 
Support product launches with go-to-market plans, launch kits, and internal training materials.

- 
Monitor trends across the AI infrastructure, cloud-native, and open-source ecosystems to surface market insights that inform GTM strategy.

- 
Support analyst and media briefings; contribute to Mirantis’ positioning in key industry conversations and reports.

- 
Represent Mirantis’ platform perspective at industry events, webinars, and community forums as a subject matter resource.

Qualifications
Required Qualifications

- 
4–7 years of experience in product marketing, technical marketing, or a closely adjacent role (solutions marketing, developer marketing, content strategy) within the cloud infrastructure, DevOps, or enterprise software space.

- 
Demonstrated ability to understand and credibly communicate complex technical concepts related to:

- 
Kubernetes, container orchestration, and cloud-native platforms

- 
AI/ML infrastructure, including model serving, inference, GPU resource management, and MLOps tooling

- 
Hybrid and multi-cloud deployment patterns across AWS, GCP, and Azure

- 
Strong writing and storytelling skills — able to produce clear, engaging content for both technical and business audiences without heavy editing.

- 
Experience developing sales enablement materials and working directly with sales and pre-sales teams.

- 
Familiarity with the competitive landscape across cloud infrastructure, AI platforms, and open-source ecosystems (e.g., Red Hat, NVIDIA, Canonical, Rancher/SUSE, HashiCorp).

- 
Comfort working in a fast-paced, distributed environment with a high degree of autonomy. 

Soft Skills and Team Fit

- 
Intellectually curious with a genuine interest in how AI infrastructure works under the hood.

- 
Strong cross-functional collaborator — comfortable engaging with engineers, product managers, and executives alike.

- 
Structured thinker who can manage multiple projects and deadlines without losing narrative coherence.

- 
Passion for open source, developer communities, and the cloud-native ecosystem.

- 
Entrepreneurial mindset with a bias toward shipping content and iterating quickly.

Nice to Have

- 
Hands-on experience with Kubernetes, MLflow, Kubeflow, Ray, or similar infrastructure tooling.

- 
Background in or exposure to AI/ML engineering, DevOps, or platform engineering.

- 
Prior experience in or familiarity with companies like RunPod, SpectroCloud, Rafay, Anyscale, Hugging Face, Together AI, or Modal.

- 
Experience supporting analyst relations or contributing to industry research and reports.

- 
CNCF ecosystem knowledge or cloud/Kubernetes certifications a plus.

Additional Information
What does Mirantis offer you?

- 
Work with an established Silicon Valley leader in the cloud infrastructure industry.

- 
Work with exceptionally passionate, talented and engaging colleagues, helping Fortune 500 and Global 2000 customers implement next-generation cloud technologies.

- 
Be a part of cutting-edge, open-source innovation.

- 
Thrive in the high-energy environment of a young company where openness, collaboration, risk-taking, and continuous growth are valued.

- 
Receive a competitive compensation package with strong benefits plan

jobs.description:
Job Title: Data Science Engineer

Position leads the data science initiative in all areas of BRR Plus’s responsibilities, including Pratt Industries and Visy, providing professional knowledge and expertise in the areas of large data analytics in support of operational improvements across all facets as designated by the Group Data Science Manager. The position will be expected to build data/machine learning products to extract valuable business insights.

 

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: (Other duties may be assigned)-

- Identify valuable data sources and automate collection processes
- Undertake preprocessing of structured and unstructured data
- Analyze large amounts of information to discover trends and patterns
- Leverage programming skills to develop automated systems that help improve business operations
- Build predictive models and machine-learning algorithms
- Present information using data visualization techniques
- Collaborate with engineering and production teams
- Assist all Pratt/Visy locations (via remote login, video conferencing, or onsite support), provide direction, training, and development support, roll out pre-developed tools, expedite installation of insightful data products
- Travel as required to support development of other Pratt/Visy locations

EDUCATION and/or EXPERIENCE -

- Experience as a Data Scientist or Data Analyst preferred
- Understanding of machine-learning and operations research preferred or demonstrated ability and desire to learn ML/AI methods to develop the desired outcome 
- Knowledge of DCS systems, Data Historians, and Data Analytics software such as: dataParc, Seeq, Python
- Analytical mind and business acumen
- Strong math skills (e.g. statistics, algebra)
- Problem-solving aptitude
- Excellent communication and presentation skills
- Post-secondary educational degree (2 to 4-year degree from college, university, technical school, military, vocational or other advanced educational institution) REQUIRED
- Candidates are preferred who offer the following:

- BS degree in Chemical Engineering, Pulp and Paper, or other engineering disciplines related to field

LANGUAGE SKILLS-

- Ability to read, analyze, and interpret technical procedures, manuals, regulations, and codes

- Ability to write business reports and correspondence

- Ability to communicate effectively with suppliers, contractors, employees and outside officials to the company

- Clear, concise oral and written communication skills

MATHEMATICAL SKILLS- 

- Ability to apply concepts of algebra and statistics along with basic math (adding, subtracting, multiplying and dividing)

REASONING ABILITY- 

- Ability to solve practical problems and deal with a variety of concrete variables in situations where only limited standardization exists

- Ability to interpret a variety of instructions furnished in written, oral, diagram, or schedule form

TECHNICAL SKILLS- 

- Ability to gather information and perform detailed analysis and problem solving

PHYSICAL DEMANDS-

- Employee is frequently required to stand; use hands to finger, handle, feel; and reach with hands and arms

- Vision requirements include: close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and ability to adjust focus

WORK ENVIRONMENT-

Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer/Veterans/Disabled.

Qualifications

Pratt Industries is America's 4th largest paper and packaging company with more than 10,000+ highly skilled green collar employees dedicated to the environment and sustainability. Pratt Industries is one of the largest corrugated packaging companies in America, with sophisticated manufacturing facilities in more than twenty states. The company operates several of the most modern, cost effective paper mills in the country, as well as a series of sheet and box plants strategically placed throughout the United States offering a wide range of creative packaging solutions. Pratt Industries is committed to recycling and the environment by harvesting the urban forest.
Pratt Industries offers competitive compensation plans with incentive opportunities, full benefits package including medical, dental, vision, life, disability, All State, Pre-Paid Legal, and 401(k) with company match, paid vacation, paid holidays, and childbearing and parental leave.

jobs.title: Automation Engineer (Shreveport, LA)
jobs.description:
Job Title: Automation Engineer

SUMMARY- 

We are seeking a highly skilled and motivated AI/Automation Engineer/Systems Integrator to join our innovative team. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in integrating advanced AI technologies into manufacturing processes, with a focus on enhancing efficiency, productivity, and quality. This role requires a hands-on professional with a deep understanding of AI, machine learning, automation systems, and systems integration.

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: (Other duties may be assigned)-

-  Integration of AI Technologies: Design, develop, and implement AI solutions to automate and optimize manufacturing processes. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate AI systems into existing workflows and machinery.
-  System Analysis and Design: Analyze current manufacturing systems to identify opportunities for AI and automation integration. Develop detailed design specifications and functional requirements.
-  Algorithm Development: Create and deploy machine learning algorithms and models to predict, analyze, and enhance manufacturing outcomes.
-  Project Management: Lead and manage AI integration projects from conception through implementation, ensuring timely delivery and adherence to project goals.
-  Data Management: Collect, preprocess, and analyze large sets of manufacturing data to train AI models. Ensure data integrity and security throughout the process.
-  Continuous Improvement: Continuously monitor and evaluate the performance of AI systems, implementing improvements and updates as necessary to maintain optimal performance.
-  Technical Support: Provide ongoing technical support and training to manufacturing teams on the use and maintenance of AI systems.

-  Technical Skills: Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, R, and C++. Experience with AI frameworks and tools such as TensorFlow, Keras, and PyTorch. Knowledge of PLCs, SCADA systems, and industrial automation protocols.
-  Analytical Skills: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex data and develop actionable insights.
-  Communication Skills: Excellent verbal and written communication skills with the ability to convey technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
-  Team Player: Ability to work effectively in a team environment and collaborate with cross-functional teams to achieve common goals.
-  Industry Certifications: Relevant certifications in AI, machine learning, or automation engineering.

Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer/Veterans/Disabled.

Pratt Industries is America's 4th largest paper and packaging company with more than 10,000+ highly skilled green collar employees dedicated to the environment and sustainability. The company operates several of the most modern, cost effective paper mills in the country, as well as a series of sheet and box plants strategically placed throughout the United States offering a wide range of creative packaging solutions. Pratt Industries is committed to recycling and the environment by harvesting the urban forest.
Pratt Industries offers competitive compensation plans with incentive opportunities, full benefits package including medical, dental, vision, life, disability, All State, Pre-Paid Legal, and 401(k) with company match, paid vacation, paid holidays, and childbearing and parental leave.

- Identify and Cultivate New Sales Opportunities: Research and identify potential clients and new market segments that can benefit from our performance-oriented digital advertising solutions. Develop strategies to engage with these prospects effectively.

Build and maintain strong, long-lasting customer relationships to foster loyalty and repeat business.
- Sales Process: Guide customers through the entire sales process--from initial contact, to proposal development and presentation, to negotiation and purchase--ensuring smooth communication and addressing any concerns or inquiries they may have along the way.
- Goal-Oriented Approach: Set ambitious sales and revenue targets and work diligently to achieve and exceed them. Monitor and analyze sales data, identifying opportunities for growth and improvement.

jobs.company: Hand & Stone Massage and Facial Spa
jobs.description:
Replies within 24 hours

Job description
Now Hiring Sales Associates for Hand and Stone San Marcos! We have a large member base and many new clients visiting our spas for the first time. Applicants must be friendly, outgoing, enthusiastic, responsible, reliable and have a professional appearance and attitude. Must feel comfortable explaining our wellness plans to new guests and recommending services, enhancements and products to our guests. Must be able to work evenings and weekends. Primary function will be to answer phones, greet guests, check clients in and out and to help run the spa. This is a long term position. If you are looking for a temporary job, this is not the right position for you.
Weekly schedule will consist of 3-4 Monday-Friday shifts from 2:30-8:30PM and 1-2 Saturday-Sunday shifts from 11:30-5:30PM.
As a front desk sales associate at this location, essential responsibilities revolve around providing excellent services to members and guests. These include:

- Assisting the spa manager with all day to day activities
- Answering busy phones and booking client appointments
- Greeting members and guest when they arrive and checking members and guests in and out for appointments
- Addressing member concerns and ensuring every guest has a great experience.
- Explaining the benefits of our membership program to new clients and selling memberships.
- Overseeing a staff of massage therapists and estheticians while on duty and making sure their duties are completed before they leave for the day.
- Educate our guests on the benefits of massage and facials and be able to explain the benefits of our infrared sauna and facial products
- Provide excellent service to our guests and members
- Maintaining impeccable spa environment throughout clinic by continuously checking therapy rooms for neatness, restocking bathroom and assisting with rotating laundry and folding towels

Qualifications

- We are looking for friendly, outgoing people with a warm personality to welcome our guests and treat them like they are family here at our spa. Having an interest in wellness and a desire to help people is a plus.
- Experience preferred in any customer service profession such as spas or medical offices, tanning, waxing or fitness centers or any other business with busy phones and interaction with guests.
- Must be dependable, reliable, responsible and have a strong work ethic.
- Must be able to handle multiple phone calls, check clients in and out and be able to multi-task and prioritize duties when it is very busy, especially at the top of the hour.
- Be comfortable working on the computer booking appointments and checking clients out.
- Have previous customer service experience (preferred).
- Work cohesively with others in a fast-paced environment but also be proactive in finding things that need to be done when things slow down. Also must be able to handle the stress of being busy, be able to find solutions when therapists call out and handle client concerns or questions.
- Be willing to learn and have a basic understanding of massage and esthetic services and modalities.
- Must be able to work evenings and weekends - We are open 9am-8pm Monday-Friday and 9am-6pm weekends.
- Professional image and appearance

We Offer:

- An excellent work environment with a great clientele in both Scripps Ranch and San Marcos near shopping, restaurants, the freeway and great parking
- 6-8 hour work schedules
- Busy, fun environment
- Paid training
- Employee massages and facials at a reduced cost, free use of infrared sauna
- Sick pay and 401K retirement plans

Work Remotely

- No

Job Type: Part-time
Salary: $19-20 per hour based on experience, $10 for each membership sold, 10% on product commissions and gift card bonus plans
Benefits:

- 401(k)
- Employee discount
- Flexible schedule

Weekly day range:

- Every weekend
- Monday to Friday

 
Education:

- High school or equivalent (Preferred)

Experience:

- Microsoft Office: 1 year (Preferred)
- Customer Service: 1 year (Preferred)

 Compensation: $19.00 - $20.00 per hour 

At Hand and Stone, Opportunity Knocks.
Over 500 locations open across the U.S.
We have opportunities for Massage Therapists, Estheticians, Spa Managers and Spa Associates at all stages of their careers. Full Time, Part Time…we can provide the perfect fit with FLEXIBLE schedules.

Hand & Stone’s focus is on creating an experience that exceeds our guests’ expectations. We treat every guest & employee with respect. We know if you have happy employees, you will have happy clients. Become part of the team and you’ll see that for yourself.

jobs.title: Talent Acquisition Partner
jobs.description:
Join our Team

At this time, Ericsson Canada Inc. does not provide immigration assistance/sponsorship now or in the future for this position.

About the Opportunity:

Ericsson is looking for a Talent Acquisition Partner – Early Careers (Intern & Co-op), based in Ottawa, to support intern and co-op hiring across Canada and the United States. This role is central to delivering high-quality early talent hiring outcomes through close collaboration with hiring managers, universities, and internal stakeholders. The successful candidate will manage end-to-end recruitment for intern and co-op positions, ensuring a seamless experience for both candidates and hiring managers while maintaining compliance with Canadian and US hiring requirements. This is a full-time, experienced-level position based in Ottawa.

What You Will Do:

- Manage full-cycle recruitment for intern and co-op roles across Canada and the US
- Collaborate with hiring managers to define hiring needs, timelines, selection criteria, and structured interview plans
- Lead intake meetings and shape recruitment strategy for early careers hiring
- Source and attract early talent through university partnerships, campus programs (with a strong focus on Canadian and/or US institutions), job boards, talent platforms, and internal pipelines
- Screen and assess candidates to build strong, diverse shortlists
- Coordinate high-volume interview processes and deliver an excellent candidate experience
- Ensure compliance with Canada and US job posting requirements, including pay transparency and sponsorship language
- Partner with Talent Acquisition Operations and HR on offers, onboarding, and hiring documentation
- Track and report on key recruiting metrics such as time-to-fill, conversion rates, and pipeline health
- Contribute to the continuous improvement of early careers hiring programs and processes

The skills you will bring:

- 3–6 years of recruiting experience, in-house or at an agency
- Experience hiring for intern, co-op, or early careers roles
- Ability to manage multiple high-volume requisitions simultaneously
- Strong stakeholder management and influencing skills
- Experience working across Canadian and/or US markets
- Familiarity with Applicant Tracking Systems (e.g., SuccessFactors, Eightfold, SmartRecruiter)
- Strong organizational and project management skills
- Experience with diversity hiring or early talent pipeline development

Why join Ericsson?

At Ericsson, you´ll have an outstanding opportunity. The chance to use your skills and imagination to push the boundaries of what´s possible. To build solutions never seen before to some of the world’s toughest problems. You´ll be challenged, but you won’t be alone. You´ll be joining a team of diverse innovators, all driven to go beyond the status quo to craft what comes next.

What happens once you apply?
Click Here to find all you need to know about what our typical hiring process looks like.

Ericsson uses a merit-based hiring approach that values people with different experiences, perspectives and skillsets. We truly believe this approach drives innovation, which is essential for our future growth. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply and realize their full potential as part of our Ericsson team. Ericsson is proud to be an Equal Opportunity employer, learn more.

If you need assistance or to request an accommodation due to a disability, please contact Ericsson at hr.direct.americas@ericsson.com. 

DISCLAIMER: The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees in this position. They are not an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties and skills required for this position, and you may be required to perform additional job tasks as assigned.

Primary country and city: Canada (CA) || Ottawa (Country/ City)

Job details: Talent Acquisition Partner

Compensation and Benefits at Ericsson
At Ericsson, we know that our people are the key to our success. We offer a competitive package to help with your individual needs and goals.

Your Pay
The salary offered is dependent on various factors including, but not limited to, location, and the candidate’s combination of job-related knowledge, qualifications, skills, education, training, and experience.

The salary range for this position is

• Ottawa: $63,500 - $83,475CAD

Short-Term Variable Compensation Plan: Your pay also includes the opportunity for an annual bonus. Actual bonus payouts are based on performance of the business against the unit’s objectives, individual performance, and the individual bonus target. Certain eligibility and pro-ration rules apply. 

Your Health

Ericsson offers excellent health benefits including the choice of 3 medical and dental plan options that allow an employee to select the level of coverage that suits their needs. Core level coverage is paid for fully by Ericsson.

Your Financial Security
We invest in both your short and long-term financial wellbeing. Ericsson’s Group Retirement & Savings Program offers an automatic 2% company contribution into the Pension Plan. Ericsson also offers a 50% match of employee’s contribution into the Registered Retirement Savings Plan, up to 8% of the employee’s contribution (maximum of 4% match). This leads to a total company contribution potential of 6%. Employees will also basic life insurance and basic accidental death and dismemberment coverage at two-times annual base pay at no cost, as well as short-term disability coverage. Employees also have the option to participate in Ericsson’s Stock Purchase Plan.

Your Time
Your work-life balance is important to us. New employees are provided a minimum of 18 days of accrued vacation, at least 3personal days, minimum 10 holidays, 1 volunteer day, and sick days. Please note paid time off is pro-rated based on the employee’s start date. Furthermore, Ericsson provides up to 10 weeks of paid maternity leave and 6 weeks of parental or adoption leave at 100% of pay.

Additional Benefits
Ericsson offers many other company-paid benefits such as financial wellness programs, educational assistance, matching gifts, wellness account, and recognition programs.

jobs.title: Account Manager, Rochester – Albany, NY
jobs.description:
About the job

An Account Manager is responsible for identifying and closing new business opportunities within established and new accounts in a geographically dispersed territory. Account Managers will focus their call activities on multi-specialty ambulatory surgery centers (ASCs) and hospitals within their assigned accounts. They serve as a true clinical partners and trusted advisors to customers, partnering with Sterile Processing Departments staff to determine solutions to their challenges.
Key Responsibilities

- Responsible for establishing and maintaining customer relationships, and overall financial performance within assigned territory through consultative and clinical value-based selling and ensuring positive Customer experiences.
- Drive market share through existing customer utilization assessments and contending for new footprint expansion while developing customer champions for ASP technologies.
- Establish relationships with hospital customers where surgical procedures are performed or influenced. Anticipate customer needs, develop innovative solutions to streamline customer workflows and think creatively to identify new revenue streams.
- Leverage business sense to identify key business levers, build value propositions, identify accurate decision makers and appropriately position solution to various customer types and call points within hospitals and local Integrated Delivery Networks (IDN), including the following: Supply Chain, Nurse Managers, Risk Management, and Sterile Processing, Materials Management, Bio-Medical/Clinical Engineering, and Infection Prevention
- Provide product demonstration, training presentations and in-servicing based upon Customer’s needs.
- Establish and maintain optimum account call frequencies which lead to better relationships with the customer, better understanding of the customer’s business and needs, and the accomplishment of the sales quota.
- Develop and implement territory sales strategy and plan for local hospitals and IDN accounts not covered by the Corporate Accounts teams.
- Responsible for local pull-through of strategic plans developed by corporate account teams.
- Routinely update sales actions plans and forecasts for Territory Quarterly Reviews and Business Plans to facilitate the purchase of ASP products and services.
- Proactively collaborates on business initiatives with internal partners such as Service, Marketing, Commercial Operations etc.
- Fosters collaboration, teamwork and partnership using effective communication.
- Leverage tools, systems, and dashboards daily as part of running a business and proactively problem solve on critical leading and lagging performance metrics to maintain a robust one-year funnel.
- Continually acquire, maintain, and expand personal understanding of features, benefits, applications and market conditions, enhancing the needs for our current and future products.
- Participate in local Chapters AORN, IAHCSMM, SGNA, and/or other relevant organizations including regional level buying groups.

Qualifications

- Bachelor’s degree or equivalent required.
- Minimum of 3 years of sales experience, or 2 years of medical sales experience, or 1 year of sales experience plus 1 year of infection prevention industry experience.
- Proven/documented success launching new products, growing product utilization, and/or protecting business from competition.
- Experience prospecting for new business and cold calling.
- Preferred experience selling to hospitals, ASC’s, and Inpatient/Outpatient Endoscopy Suites.
- Candidates must possess a valid driver's license issued in the United States.
- The ability to travel based on the requirements of the role.
- Must live in or be willing to relocate within defined territory.

Fortive Corporation Overview
Fortive’s essential technology makes the world safer and more productive. We accelerate transformation in high-impact fields like workplace safety, build environments, and healthcare.
We are a global industrial technology innovator with a startup spirit. Our forward-looking companies lead the way in healthcare sterilization, industrial safety, predictive maintenance, and other mission-critical solutions. We’re a force for progress, working alongside our customers and partners to solve challenges on a global scale, from workplace safety in the most demanding conditions to advanced technologies that help providers focus on exceptional patient care.
We are a diverse team 10,000 strong, united by a dynamic, inclusive culture and energized by limitless learning and growth. We use the proven Fortive Business System (FBS) to accelerate our positive impact.
At Fortive, we believe in you. We believe in your potential—your ability to learn, grow, and make a difference.
At Fortive, we believe in us. We believe in the power of people working together to solve problems no one could solve alone.
At Fortive, we believe in growth. We’re honest about what’s working and what isn’t, and we never stop improving and innovating.
Fortive: For you, for us, for growth.
About Advanced Sterilization Products
ASP is a leading global provider of innovative sterilization and disinfection solutions, and a pioneer of low-temperature hydrogen peroxide sterilization technology. ASP’s mission is to protect patients during their most critical moments. We support healthcare facilities in the fight to protect patients against hospital acquired infections, which are a leading cause of morbidity and mortality. ASP’s products, which are sold globally, include the STERRAD system for sterilizing instruments and the EVOTECH and ENDOCLENS systems for endoscope reprocessing and cleaning.
